explanation: Axial CBCT sections show how many bony walls remain around an infrabony defect. A three-wall defect (three remaining walls) is the classic morphology assessed on axial imaging and has the best regenerative prognosis.

Koilonychia (spoon nails), smooth red tongue, pallor, and microcytic anemia (low RBC and low MCV) point to iron-deficiency anemia. Pernicious anemia is macrocytic (high MCV) from B12 deficiency, so it is excluded.

Severe xerostomia from minor salivary disease makes mucosa-borne RPDs poorly retained and traumatic. Tooth- or implant-supported fixed options avoid reliance on saliva; implant-supported partial FPD is preferred for replacing two premolars when abutments/implants allow.

Sarcoidosis on minor salivary-gland biopsy shows noncaseating granulomatous (granulomatous/granulation-type) inflammation. Fibrosis, acanthosis, and acinar hypertrophy are not the diagnostic histologic pattern.

Before a definitive impression for a new RPD, tissues distorted by an old prosthesis need rest. Leaving the old RPD out about 24 hours (tissue rest) is the common exam interval among the listed choices so mucosa can rebound before impression.
Maxillary major connectors should clear free gingival margins by about 6 mm to avoid plaque trapping and gingival inflammation. About 3 mm is the usual mandibular clearance figure, not maxillary.

Myxedema coma is severe hypothyroidism with altered mental status; hypothermia is the hallmark early/clinical sign from failed thermoregulation. Bradycardia occurs but hypothermia is classically cited as the key first/cardinal finding.

After scaling and root planing, inflamed connective tissue and long junctional epithelium remodel: pocket depth and inflammatory infiltrate first regress, then collagen organization and attachment quality increase during healing.

Acute agitation with an unused/mentioned inhaler and no clear asthma story fits hyperventilation syndrome (anxiety-driven tachypnea, lightheadedness, aggression/panic). Hypoglycemia needs diabetes context; hypothyroidism is not an acute aggressive attack.

Cavit is a temporary zinc oxide–based endodontic provisional only. It lacks strength, seal longevity, and wear resistance for definitive restoration and must be replaced with a proper temporary or permanent material.

Primary upper anterior that darkens after trauma then spontaneously lightens suggests transient pulp hemorrhage/resorption of pigment with recovering vitality. Observation/follow-up with vitality testing is preferred over immediate invasive treatment.

When IE prophylaxis is indicated and the patient cannot take oral meds, parenteral ampicillin (IM/IV, typically 2 g) is the standard substitute. Oral amoxicillin, azithromycin, or clindamycin apply only if oral dosing is possible.

Rigidly connecting a natural tooth to an implant loads the tooth’s PDL differently from the osseointegrated implant. Relative intrusion or mobility of the tooth tends to break the cement seal on the tooth abutment rather than equally stressing both units.

Multiple caries and abscesses in a young child define severe early childhood caries as the clinical dental diagnosis. Neglect may be a social risk factor, but the question asks for the disease diagnosis, not the type of maltreatment.
Toughness is the energy absorbed per unit volume up to fracture (area under the stress–strain curve). Modulus of elasticity is stiffness (slope of the elastic portion), not total energy to break.

Plaque index ~40% with BOP ~20% means substantial plaque remains despite some cleaning effort and moderate inflammation. That pattern matches inadequate technique with attempted brushing rather than total non-brushing or perfect technique without brushing.

Chronic mouth breathing often links to maxillary constriction and crossbite/narrow arch. Orthopedic maxillary expansion improves nasal airway space and transverse arch form as the main skeletal/orthodontic treatment hinge.

Among the garbled options, the item targets HBV transmission context. HBV is blood- and body-fluid–borne (percutaneous/mucosal), not a classic respiratory droplet pathogen like influenza; the keyed option is the only HBV-related choice in this bank item.

Carbohydrate-driven demineralization under or around a prosthesis produces white-spot/caries lesions often described with denture hygiene problems. Caries is the pathologic process; diet investigation is adjunctive history, not the lesion type itself.

In PFM design, porcelain is brittle in tension but strong in compression; the metal substructure is shaped so porcelain is kept under compressive loading during function, reducing tensile fracture of the veneer.

After traumatic loss of an upper central (#21), a bone-level implant allows platform placement for soft-tissue thickness and esthetic emergence in the anterior maxilla. Tissue-level designs are less flexible for high-smile esthetic zones.

Generalized brown intrinsic discoloration that fluoresces yellow under ultraviolet light is classic for tetracycline staining during tooth formation. Extrinsic stains and most other intrinsic defects lack this UV fluorescence pattern.

Intact restoration with tight contacts, normal probing depth, food impaction, and gingival pain suggests closed embrasures (decreased embrasure space) that trap food, not periodontal pocketing or plaque-induced disease alone.

Hormonal changes of the menstrual cycle increase gingival vascularity and gingival crevicular fluid flow, producing transient gingivitis-like signs without necessarily changing pocket depth from true attachment loss.

Hypoplasia limited to incisal tips and cusp tips of premolars/molars marks a short systemic insult during early postnatal/perinatal crown formation (chronologic enamel hypoplasia from perinatal deficiency/illness), not generalized fluorosis or full amelogenesis imperfecta.

The angle former is a hand instrument used to sharpen internal line angles, place retention features in dentin, and place enamel bevels—especially in cast gold cavity preparations. It is not a finishing strip or porcelain etchant.

Perineural invasion on histology of a salivary neoplasm is a signature of adenoid cystic carcinoma, explaining pain and local spread along nerves. Mucoepidermoid carcinoma more often shows cystic/mucous and epidermoid cells without that classic perineural predilection.

Generalized hypocalcified enamel needs masking of color and reinforcement of structure; composite veneers restore esthetics and protect weak enamel. Microabrasion/bleaching alone do not adequately treat substantial hypocalcification.

Legal and clinical quality of dental records hinges first on completeness: history, findings, diagnosis, consent, treatment, and follow-up must all be present. Objectivity and specificity matter but incomplete notes fail documentation standards even if tidy.

A nonvital or endodontically symptomatic tooth with an apical abscess and radiographic apical pathosis is primary endodontic disease. Primary periodontal disease would show deep pockets and crestal bone loss as the driver without a primary pulp source.
